waypoints.name: North Face Viewpoint

itinerary.mapNote: Easy boardwalk start through old-growth. First sea stacks. 0–30m.
Most popular camp. Waterfall drops straight to beach. 0m.
Surge channels, ladders, tide-dependent beaches. 0m.
Near Carmanah Lighthouse. Giant Sitka spruce grove. 0m.
Sheltered bay. Sandstone shelves and sea caves. 0m.
Final beach camp before steep climb inland. 0m.
Ferry across Gordon River. Port Renfrew trailhead. 0m.

waypoints.name: Pachena Bay (Start)
waypoints.note: Northern trailhead. Pachena Lighthouse. Pacific Rim National Park.

waypoints.name: Tsusiat Falls
waypoints.note: Waterfall drops directly onto beach. Most iconic photo on WCT.

waypoints.name: Carmanah Lighthouse
waypoints.note: Active lighthouse on exposed headland. Heritage buildings.

waypoints.name: Carmanah Giant Sitka
waypoints.note: Ancient Sitka spruce grove. Trees over 800 years old, 95m tall.

waypoints.name: Gordon River (Finish)
waypoints.note: Southern trailhead. Ferry crossing. Port Renfrew access.

itinerary.mapNote: Imlil start. Mule path over first pass. 2279m.
Tacheddirt village passed. High shepherd pastures. 2900m.
Remote Agoundis valley. Berber villages. 1800m.
Highest natural lake in High Atlas. 2313m.
CAF/Club Alpin hut below summit. 3207m.
Pre-dawn Toubkal 4167m summit. Descent to Imlil. 1740m.

waypoints.name: Jbel Toubkal Summit
waypoints.note: Highest peak in North Africa. 7–8hr round trip from refuge.

waypoints.name: Lac d'Ifni
waypoints.note: Highest natural lake in High Atlas. Remote and beautiful.

waypoints.name: Imlil Village
waypoints.note: Trek gateway. Mule market. Berber guesthouses. Tizi n'Test view.

waypoints.name: Tizi n'Ouanoums
waypoints.note: High pass on approach to Toubkal. Summit plateau edge.

waypoints.name: Toubkal Refuge (CAF)
waypoints.note: Last hut before summit. Crowded in summer. Stunning position.

itinerary.mapNote: Longtong Pa start. Milk Lake 4700m. Alpine meadows. 4700m.
Five Colour Lake. Circuit around three sacred peaks. 3800m.

waypoints.name: Chenrezig (Xiannairi)
waypoints.note: Sacred peak of compassion. One of three holy Yading peaks.

waypoints.name: Jambeyang (Yangmaiyong)
waypoints.note: Sacred peak of wisdom. Sharpest of the three.

waypoints.name: Chanadorje (Xianuoduoji)
waypoints.note: Sacred peak of strength. Kora circumnavigates all three.

waypoints.name: Milk Lake (Nailin Hai)
waypoints.note: Turquoise glacial lake. Sacred. Best reflection of Chenrezig.

waypoints.name: Five Colour Lake
waypoints.note: Multi-hued glacial lake. Highest point of kora circuit.

itinerary.mapNote: Aerial Tramway bypass to valley floor, then climb to stunning Marion Lake. 2747m.

itinerary.route: Marion Lake to Death Canyon Shelf
itinerary.mapNote: Dramatic shelf camp with sweeping Teton views. Exposed camping. 2865m.

itinerary.route: Death Canyon Shelf to Alaska Basin
itinerary.mapNote: Wildflower meadows and the extraordinary Alaska Basin basin camps. 3008m.

itinerary.route: Alaska Basin to South Fork Cascade
itinerary.mapNote: Cross Hurricane Pass with Grand Teton's full north face revealed. 2987m.

itinerary.route: South Fork to Leigh Lake Trailhead
itinerary.mapNote: Final descent through ancient moraines to the valley floor. 2073m.

waypoints.name: Hurricane Pass
waypoints.note: Highest point on the trail. Grand Teton's north face fills the sky.

waypoints.name: Grand Teton
waypoints.note: Highest peak in the Teton Range. Dominates every viewpoint on the trail.

waypoints.name: Alaska Basin
waypoints.note: Wildflower paradise — best alpine meadows in Wyoming.

waypoints.name: Death Canyon Shelf
waypoints.note: Dramatic shelf with 50km views across the range.

itinerary.route: Big Sandy Lodge to Big Sandy Lake
itinerary.mapNote: Classic Wyoming trail start. Pine forests to open granite. 2926m.

itinerary.route: Big Sandy Lake to Cirque of Towers
itinerary.mapNote: Destination highlight — granite spires surrounding a perfect alpine lake. 3200m.

itinerary.route: Cirque of Towers to Lonesome Lake
itinerary.mapNote: Cross Jackass Pass, descend to impossibly blue Lonesome Lake. 3050m.
Off-trail navigation begins in earnest. Granite slabs and boulder fields. 3100m.
Spectacular high route traversing unnamed passes. Route-finding essential. 3000m.
Final descent on good trail. Fremont Lake visible below. 2490m.

waypoints.name: Cirque of Towers
waypoints.note: One of America's most dramatic alpine cirques. 10 granite spires.

waypoints.name: Jackass Pass
waypoints.note: Gateway to the Cirque. Views back to Wind River peaks.

waypoints.name: Gannet Peak
waypoints.note: Wyoming's highest point. Visible from high points on the route.

waypoints.name: Lonesome Lake
waypoints.note: Electric blue glacial lake below sheer granite walls.

itinerary.mapNote: Off-trail immediately. Climb through boulder fields to first high lake. 2987m.

itinerary.route: Grouse Lake to Upper Gardiner Basin
itinerary.mapNote: Full off-trail day. Cross Window Pass. Vast granite wilderness. 3400m.
JMT briefly joined at Rae Lakes. Leave trail immediately after. 3200m.
Remote basin rarely visited. Dragon Peak dominates skyline. 3350m.
The White Wall — most dramatic off-trail section of the route. 3300m.
Classic Sierra glacially-carved recess. Perfect granite camping. 3100m.
High traverse above Mono Creek. Views extend to distant ranges. 3250m.
Pass over Silver Divide. JMT rejoined briefly at Purple Lake. 3180m.

itinerary.route: Duck Lake to Mammoth Pass TH
itinerary.mapNote: Descent through lodgepole pines to Mammoth Lakes trailhead. 2600m.

waypoints.name: Window Peak
waypoints.note: Critical navigation landmark on Day 2. 360° Sierra panorama.

waypoints.name: Dragon Peak
waypoints.note: Iconic jagged summit visible from Lake Basin.

waypoints.name: Muro Blanco
waypoints.note: The White Wall — granite swept clean of soil. Most dramatic section.

waypoints.name: Mount Whitney
waypoints.note: Southern terminus landmark. Highest peak in contiguous USA.

itinerary.route: Maligne Lake TH to Little Shovel Pass
itinerary.mapNote: Leave Maligne Lake behind, climb through subalpine to first high camp. 2150m.

itinerary.route: Little Shovel to Shovel Pass Camp
itinerary.mapNote: The famous ridge traverse. Panoramic views of Jasper's high country. 2350m.

itinerary.route: Shovel Pass to Signal Mountain TH
itinerary.mapNote: Descend from alpine to valley through old-growth forest. 1670m.

waypoints.name: Shovel Pass
waypoints.note: Highest point of the trail. Classic Jasper viewpoint. 2560m.

waypoints.name: Little Shovel Pass
waypoints.note: First high point. Gateway to the main ridge walk.

waypoints.name: Maligne Lake
waypoints.note: Iconic Canadian Rockies lake. Trail start. Most photographed in Jasper.

waypoints.name: Curator Lake
waypoints.note: Remote alpine lake below Shovel Pass. Perfect reflection pool.

itinerary.route: Floe Lake TH to Floe Lake
itinerary.mapNote: Iconic Kootenay campsite directly below the Rockwall. 2010m.
Cross Numa Pass. First full view of the 30km limestone wall. 1900m.
Helmet Falls — one of Canada's tallest waterfalls. 1950m.

itinerary.route: Helmet Falls to Paint Pots TH
itinerary.mapNote: Ochre natural mineral springs at trail end. Kootenay NP exit. 1020m.

waypoints.name: Rockwall
waypoints.note: 30km continuous limestone escarpment — most dramatic wall in Canadian Rockies.

waypoints.name: Numa Pass
waypoints.note: Highest point on trail. The full Rockwall reveals itself here.

waypoints.name: Helmet Falls
waypoints.note: 330m free-fall waterfall. One of Canada's tallest. Spectacular camp nearby.

waypoints.name: Floe Lake
waypoints.note: Turquoise glacial lake directly below sheer Rockwall face.

itinerary.mapNote: Descend dramatically into the Apurímac canyon. Tropical heat. 1550m.
Cross the Apurímac River suspension bridge. Remote canyon camp. 1480m.
Long steep climb to the ruins. The citadel emerges from the cloud forest. 3085m.
Full morning exploring 'the other Machu Picchu'. Llama terraces. 2850m.
Long return ascent out of the canyon. Stunning views all the way. 2900m.

waypoints.name: Choquequirao Citadel
waypoints.note: Major Inca royal estate. Only 30% excavated. Far fewer crowds than Machu Picchu.

waypoints.name: Apurímac Canyon
waypoints.note: One of the world's deepest canyons. The Inca called it 'God of speech'.

waypoints.name: Llama Terraces
waypoints.note: Unique white llama inlaid in the terracing — only found at Choquequirao.

waypoints.name: Capuliyoq Pass
waypoints.note: Gateway views into the canyon. Cachora village visible below.

itinerary.mapNote: Start in agricultural highlands. Small indigenous Kichwa villages. 2900m.
Cloud forest descent and ascent. Andean condors often visible. 3180m.
Climb to the volcanic crater. Turquoise lake fills the caldera. 3914m.
Hike down to crater lake, explore. Bus back to Latacunga. 3200m.

waypoints.name: Quilotoa Crater Lake
waypoints.note: Electric turquoise volcanic crater lake. One of Ecuador's most dramatic sights.

waypoints.name: Chugchilán
waypoints.note: Cloud forest village. Famous hostel. Andean condors in surrounding valleys.

waypoints.name: Isinliví
waypoints.note: Traditional woodcarving village. Excellent base for Day 1.

waypoints.name: Toachi Canyon
waypoints.note: Deep canyon visible from trail. Subtropical forest far below.

itinerary.route: Puerto Williams to Laguna del Salto
itinerary.mapNote: Town exit, immediate subantarctic forest. Wind picks up fast. 280m.

itinerary.route: Laguna del Salto to Laguna Los Dientes
itinerary.mapNote: First views of the Dientes spires. Most dramatic section. 430m.

itinerary.route: Laguna Los Dientes to Laguna Escondida
itinerary.mapNote: Cross Col sur Paso Virginia. Beech forest camp. 350m.
Southernmost section. Exposed moorland. Serious wind exposure. 450m.
Final descent to world's southernmost city. Circuit complete. 50m.

waypoints.name: Dientes de Navarino
waypoints.note: The 'Teeth of Navarino'. Southernmost trekking peaks in the world.

waypoints.name: Paso Virginia
waypoints.note: Main col crossing. Exposed to Fuegian winds. Usually snowfield.

waypoints.name: Beagle Channel View
waypoints.note: Views across Beagle Channel to Argentine Tierra del Fuego.

waypoints.name: Puerto Williams
waypoints.note: World's southernmost city. Population ~2,800. End of the trail.

itinerary.route: Villa Cerro Castillo to Laguna Desierta
itinerary.mapNote: Steep climb from Patagonian steppe to first views of the castle spires. 1550m.

itinerary.route: Laguna Desierta to Laguna Cerro Castillo
itinerary.mapNote: Glacier lagoon below the basalt towers. Icebergs calving constantly. 1400m.

itinerary.route: Laguna Cerro Castillo to Campamento Neozelandes
itinerary.mapNote: Cross Paso Cerro Castillo with full tower panorama. 1600m.

itinerary.route: Campamento Neozelandes to Villa Bahía Murta
itinerary.mapNote: Descend to Carretera Austral and General Carrera Lake. 200m.

waypoints.name: Cerro Castillo
waypoints.note: The castle — basalt spires hanging over a hanging glacier. Patagonia's hidden gem.

waypoints.name: Lago Cerro Castillo
waypoints.note: Turquoise glacial lake with calving ice from Glaciar Cerro Castillo.

waypoints.name: Paso Cerro Castillo
waypoints.note: Full panoramic view of the castle from above. Best photography point.

waypoints.name: General Carrera Lake
waypoints.note: World's second largest lake in South America. Marble Caves nearby.

itinerary.mapNote: Leave Ben Nevis behind. Loch Shiel and the famous viaduct. 300m.
Remote glen walking. No waymarks. Navigation essential from here. 50m.
Famous bothy at the head of Loch Nevis. Only accessible by foot or boat. 50m.
Wild crossing through the Rough Bounds. Scotland's most remote country. 100m.
Through Torridon. Ancient 750-million-year-old Torridonian sandstone. 400m.
Cross the remote Fisherfield — Scotland's last great wilderness. 300m.
Assynt landscape — isolated mountains rising from flat boggy moorland. 200m.
The end. Britain's most remote lighthouse. Journey complete. 122m.

waypoints.name: Cape Wrath Lighthouse
waypoints.note: Britain's most northwesterly point. End of the trail.

waypoints.name: Fisherfield
waypoints.note: Scotland's last great wilderness. No roads. Complete solitude.

waypoints.name: Sourlies Bothy
waypoints.note: Famous bothie. Only way in is on foot or by boat from Mallaig.

waypoints.name: Liathach
waypoints.note: Torridon's iconic ridge. 750-million-year-old red sandstone.

itinerary.mapNote: Leave Zermatt. First Matterhorn views. Gornergletscher panorama. 2222m.
Cross into Italy via Felik Pass. Highest overnight on the route. 3585m.
Long Italian descent into the Valsesia valley. Culture shift. 1191m.
Long climb back to altitude. Italian hut culture. 2970m.
Swiss/Italian mountain village below Monte Rosa's massive East Face. 1327m.

itinerary.route: Macugnaga to Rif. Città di Novara
itinerary.mapNote: Climb out of Macugnaga. Views of the East Face grow with every step. 3020m.
Cross into Switzerland. Mattmark reservoir. 1672m.
Traverse high above the Saas Valley. Weissmies and Alphubel. 3100m.
Descent to the Matter Valley. Final valley transit. 1127m.
Return to Zermatt. Matterhorn welcomes you back. 1608m.

waypoints.name: Monte Rosa
waypoints.note: Switzerland's highest peak. Dominates the circuit.

waypoints.name: Matterhorn
waypoints.note: Zermatt's iconic pyramid. Start and finish backdrop.

waypoints.name: Felik Pass
waypoints.note: Italy-Switzerland border crossing. Magnificent glacier views.

waypoints.name: Monte Rosa East Face
waypoints.note: One of the Alps' great walls — 2500m vertical drop visible from Macugnaga.

itinerary.mapNote: Climb from Stubaital valley to first hut. Panoramic terrace views. 2237m.
Traverse the Wilde Grube. Wild high-alpine terrain. 2413m.
Highest fixed point on trail. Glacier views in every direction. 2369m.
Cross the Feuerstein saddle. Waterfall scenery on descent. 1884m.
Below Wilder Freiger glacier. Classic Stubai high camp. 2280m.
Final high traverse. Zugspitze visible in clear conditions. 2302m.
Gondola option or walk out. Circuit complete. 950m.

waypoints.name: Wilder Pfaff
waypoints.note: Stubai's classic summit ascent from Dresdner Hütte.

waypoints.name: Stubaier Gletscher
waypoints.note: Austria's largest easily-accessible glacier. Visible from multiple huts.

waypoints.name: Innsbrucker Hütte
waypoints.note: Most spectacular hut position on the circuit.

waypoints.name: Wilder Freiger
waypoints.note: Highest peak in the southern Stubai. Classic glacier tour summit.

itinerary.mapNote: Zillertal valley start. Climb to first hut above the treeline. 2498m.
The famous suspended bridge. Schlegeis reservoir views. 2388m.
Glacier traverses. Crevasse awareness needed. 2295m.
The high route above Floitengrund valley. Most remote section. 2227m.
Dramatic Stillupgrund crossing. Hanging valley scenery. 2178m.
Cross into the lower Zillertal system. Final hut. 2238m.
Descent to the Zillertal's main valley town. Route complete. 633m.

waypoints.name: Olperer
waypoints.note: Zillertal's signature peak. Visible from the famous suspension bridge.

waypoints.name: Olperer Suspension Bridge
waypoints.note: Instagram's most photographed bridge. 100m above Schlegeis reservoir.

waypoints.name: Schlegeis Reservoir
waypoints.note: Turquoise high-altitude reservoir. Road accessible but stunning.

waypoints.name: Großer Möseler
waypoints.note: Second highest Zillertal peak. Summit panorama includes Dolomites.

itinerary.mapNote: Arrive by ferry to the abandoned fishing village. Arctic fox immediately visible. 50m.
Cross to Hornvík bay. Pass Hornbjarg cliffs with the seabird colonies. 200m.
The dramatic high crossing. Views to Greenland on clear days. 300m.
Descend into abandoned farm fjord. Pure solitude. 100m.
Final fjord crossing. Ferry returns to Isafjordur. 50m.

waypoints.name: Hornbjarg Cliffs
waypoints.note: Iceland's most dramatic bird cliffs. Half a million seabirds nest here.

waypoints.name: Hesteyri
waypoints.note: Abandoned doctor's house. Last inhabited 1952. Arctic fox colony now.

waypoints.name: Kálfatindar
waypoints.note: Highest point of the traverse. Greenland visible in clear conditions.

waypoints.name: Hornvík
waypoints.note: Most dramatic beach in Iceland. Backed by 500m cliffs.

itinerary.mapNote: Remote East Nepal start. Tamur River valley. Rhododendron forests. 1270m.
River junction camp. Traditional Limbu villages. 1660m.
Climbing into higher country. Kanchenjunga begins appearing. 2490m.
Dramatic cliffs and waterfalls. High alpine zone begins. 3730m.
Main supply village. Sherpa/Tibetan culture. Last teahouses. 3595m.
Valley narrows. Kanchenjunga North Face begins to dominate. 4050m.
High plateau camp. Glacier views. Altitude hits hard. 4790m.

itinerary.route: Lhonak to North Base Camp + return
itinerary.mapNote: North Base Camp — the world's third highest peak base. 5143m.
Long descent day. Body recovering from altitude. 3595m.
Cross-valley route. Dramatic high pass to southern approach. 4290m.
Descend to southern valley. Very different vegetation and culture. 3870m.

itinerary.route: Tseram to South Base Camp + return
itinerary.mapNote: South Base Camp — classic cirque. Yalung Glacier. 4580m.
Descend Yalung valley. Bamboo forest. Rhododendron. 2995m.
Final valley walk. Jeep possible for last section. 1820m.

waypoints.name: Kanchenjunga
waypoints.note: World's third highest peak. Sacred to local Sikkimese and Nepali people.

waypoints.name: North Base Camp
waypoints.note: Kanchenjunga North Base Camp. Remote and rarely visited.

waypoints.name: South Base Camp
waypoints.note: Classic Yalung Glacier cirque. Dramatic south face views.

waypoints.name: Sele La Pass
waypoints.note: Crossing between north and south approaches. Outstanding panorama.

itinerary.mapNote: Gateway to Upper Mustang. Ancient walled town on the Kali Gandaki. 2810m.
Leave Lower Mustang. Wind-carved ochre badlands begin. 3050m.
Plateau landscape. Blue sky above terracotta earth. 3800m.
The walled capital of the ancient Kingdom of Mustang. 3840m.
Explore the royal palace, monasteries, cave cities. 3840m.
Red cliff village. Return route via different valleys. 3820m.
Long mani wall — one of the longest in Nepal. 3520m.
Second largest town. 500-year-old dzong fortress. 3640m.
Descend back through the dramatic Mustang plateau. 2810m.
Return to Jomsom. Flight or jeep track to Pokhara. 2720m.

waypoints.name: Lo Manthang
waypoints.note: Ancient walled capital of the Kingdom of Mustang. Still partially restricted.

waypoints.name: Luri Gompa
waypoints.note: Ancient cave monastery. 800-year-old murals. Hidden valley location.

waypoints.name: Chungsi Cave
waypoints.note: Pilgrimage cave monastery. 1,000 years of butter lamp offerings.

waypoints.name: Kali Gandaki
waypoints.note: World's deepest gorge between Annapurna and Dhaulagiri.

itinerary.mapNote: Trailhead village. Remote eastern Nepal. Rice paddies give way to forest. 1480m.
Last permanent settlement. 1400m climb through terraced fields. 2100m.
Strenuous climb from subtropical to alpine. Stunning views behind. 3559m.
Enter the Barun Valley. High yak pastures. First Makalu glimpse. 3630m.
The Barun Valley opens up. Makalu's south face dominates. 3900m.
Last major camp before base camp. Stunning valley position. 4615m.
The fifth highest peak on Earth from below. Complete isolation. 5550m.
Explore glacier margins. Incredible views into Tibet. 5550m.
Descent begins. Body grateful for dropping altitude. 4615m.
Long valley descent. Barun River rushing below. 3630m.
Big descent day. Subtropical warmth returns. 2100m.
Final walk out. Jeep to Tumlingtar for flight. 950m.

waypoints.name: Makalu
waypoints.note: World's fifth highest peak. The pyramid-shaped giant of eastern Nepal.

waypoints.name: Makalu Base Camp
waypoints.note: Remote and rarely visited. One of the most spectacular in the Himalaya.

waypoints.name: Barun Valley
waypoints.note: Protected valley — one of the last pristine Himalayan valleys remaining.

waypoints.name: Baruntse
waypoints.note: Neighbouring 7000m peak visible from the route. Popular mountaineering objective.

itinerary.mapNote: Road walk start. Myagdi River. Teahouses thin quickly. 1180m.
River gorge narrows. Suspension bridges. Tropical forest. 1840m.
Last major settlement. Stock up. Route becomes remote. 2130m.
Dhaulagiri's south face begins to loom. 3010m.
Historic base camp of the first Dhaulagiri ascent. 3660m.

itinerary.route: Italian BC to Dhaulagiri Base Camp
itinerary.mapNote: The seventh highest mountain from below. Immense scale. 4750m.
The circuit's highest point. Crevassed glacier approach. 5360m.
Over the pass into the extraordinary hidden plateau. 4760m.
Epic descent to the Kali Gandaki. Apple orchards. Civilisation. 2665m.
Short valley day. Rest after the circuit's hardest section. 2560m.
Annapurna Circuit junction. Trek connects here. 2530m.
Famous hot springs. Soak tired legs. 1190m.
Trail or bus to Beni. Circuit complete. 900m.
Rest day built in for weather or altitude adjustment. 1190m.

waypoints.name: Dhaulagiri
waypoints.note: World's seventh highest mountain. Meaning 'dazzling white mountain'.

waypoints.name: French Pass
waypoints.note: Most demanding crossing on the circuit. Crevasse navigation required.

waypoints.name: Hidden Valley
waypoints.note: High plateau hidden behind Dhaulagiri. Completely isolated from the world.

waypoints.name: Italian Base Camp
waypoints.note: Original 1960 French/Swiss base camp. Historic mountaineering site.

itinerary.mapNote: Arrive Leh. Mandatory rest day at 3500m before trek begins. 3500m.
Walk on or alongside the frozen Zanskar River. Ice camping begins. 3280m.
The famous overhang cave. River ice thickens. Stunning canyon walls. 3230m.
Nerak waterfall — frozen solid, 30m of blue ice. Magical campsite. 3180m.
Visit Nerak village. Frozen waterfall exploration. Cultural exchange. 3200m.
Return journey begins. Ice conditions change daily. 3230m.
River return. Ice sheet walking. Canyon silence. 3280m.
Exit trek. Drive back to Leh along Indus Valley. 3280m.

waypoints.name: Nerak Waterfall
waypoints.note: 30m frozen waterfall. Completely solid in January-February. Iconic image.

waypoints.name: Zanskar Gorge
waypoints.note: 300m vertical walls above frozen river. Most dramatic canyon in Ladakh.

waypoints.name: Tibb Cave
waypoints.note: Natural overhang used as campsite. Locals and trekkers share the space.

waypoints.name: Leh Palace
waypoints.note: 17th century palace overlooking Leh. Acclimatisation day visit.

itinerary.route: Pak Tam Chung to Kei Ling Ha Hoi
itinerary.mapNote: Sai Kung coastline. Volcanic hexagonal rock columns. Clear water bays. 400m.

itinerary.route: Kei Ling Ha to Gilwell Camp
itinerary.mapNote: Cross Sharp Peak and Nam Sheong. Remote Sai Kung interior. 350m.

itinerary.route: Gilwell Camp to Shui Long Wo
itinerary.mapNote: Traverse Maclehose Ridge. City skyline appears on horizon. 400m.

itinerary.route: Shui Long Wo to Lead Mine Pass
itinerary.mapNote: Cross the reservoirs catchment. Lion Rock visible ahead. 400m.

itinerary.route: Lead Mine Pass to Tuen Mun
itinerary.mapNote: Final section past Lead Mine Pass. Highest point at Needle Hill. 957m.

waypoints.name: Sharp Peak
waypoints.note: Sai Kung's dramatic rocky fin. Most iconic summit on the trail.

waypoints.name: High Island Reservoir
waypoints.note: Sea wall reservoir. Hexagonal volcanic columns. Geological wonder.

waypoints.name: Needle Hill
waypoints.note: Highest point on the trail. Views to Shenzhen skyline.

waypoints.name: Lion Rock
waypoints.note: Symbol of Hong Kong. Silhouette visible from downtown.

itinerary.mapNote: Through giraffe and buffalo country in Arusha NP. Forest to heath zone. 2514m.
Moorland zone. Colobus monkeys. First views of the ash cone crater. 3570m.
Pre-dawn start. Dramatic knife-edge ridge to Socialist Peak summit. 4566m.
Full descent through all vegetation zones. Wildlife on way out. 1524m.

waypoints.name: Socialist Peak
waypoints.note: Meru summit. Named by Julius Nyerere. Views of Kilimanjaro at sunrise.

waypoints.name: Ash Cone
waypoints.note: 1910 eruption cone inside the main crater. Eerie volcanic landscape.

waypoints.name: Momella Lakes
waypoints.note: Alkaline lakes with flamingos. Hippos at dawn.

waypoints.name: Little Meru
waypoints.note: Secondary summit. Used as acclimatisation point before main ascent.

itinerary.mapNote: Lakeshore then steep climb to bush-line. Luxmore Caves. Panoramic ridge. 1472m.

itinerary.route: Luxmore Hut to Iris Burn Hut
itinerary.mapNote: Spectacular alpine ridge walk. Views of Fiordland peaks and lakes. 570m.
Manapouri lakeside hut. Beech forest. Remote and tranquil. 200m.
Flat lakeside walk back. Rainbow trout visible in crystal water. 210m.

waypoints.name: Mount Luxmore
waypoints.note: Kepler summit. Alpine meadows and one of NZ's great ridge walks.

waypoints.name: Luxmore Caves
waypoints.note: Limestone cave system. Large colony of cave weta inside.

waypoints.name: Lake Te Anau
waypoints.note: NZ's largest South Island lake. Gateway to Fiordland.

waypoints.name: Fiordland
waypoints.note: 20,000km² of raw Fiordland wilderness visible from the ridge.

itinerary.mapNote: Access via jet boat. NZ's deepest lake. Beech forest. Pure wilderness. 330m.
Muddy Fiordland terrain begins immediately. Track often submerged. 400m.

itinerary.route: Halfway Hut to Loch Maree Hut
itinerary.mapNote: Remote loch camp. No shortcuts, no alternatives, no mobile signal. 400m.

itinerary.route: Loch Maree to Lake Roe Hut
itinerary.mapNote: Alpine crossing. Views finally. Most dramatic section. 600m.
Exposed ridge bivouac. Weather dependent — be prepared to wait. 600m.
Descend to Dusky Sound. Water taxi pre-booked from here. 0m (sea level).
Water taxi to Lake Manapouri, then bus to Te Anau. Civilisation. 0m.

waypoints.name: Lake Hauroko
waypoints.note: New Zealand's deepest lake. Jet boat access. Complete wilderness.

waypoints.name: Dusky Sound
waypoints.note: One of Fiordland's most remote fiords. Accessed only by this trail or boat.

waypoints.name: Loch Maree
waypoints.note: Remote loch deep in the Fiordland interior. No road access ever.

waypoints.name: Mount Seaforth
waypoints.note: Highest point visible from the route. Classic Fiordland dark pyramid.

itinerary.route: Georgia — Springer Mountain to NC Border
itinerary.mapNote: Section 1/8 · Georgia · Southern terminus Springer Mountain. Blood Mountain 1372m — highest GA point. 78 miles.

itinerary.route: Great Smoky Mountains — Fontana Dam to Davenport Gap
itinerary.overnight: Mount Collins Shelter / Cosby Knob Shelter
itinerary.mapNote: Section 2/8 · GSMNP · Clingmans Dome 2025m — highest point on entire AT. Shelter reservations required. 110 miles.

itinerary.route: Roan Highlands — Davenport Gap to Damascus VA
itinerary.overnight: Roan High Knob Shelter / Damascus hostel
itinerary.mapNote: Section 3/8 · TN/NC border · Roan Mountain balds 1916m. Grassy ridge walks. Damascus — the friendliest trail town. 180 miles.
Section 4/8 · Southern VA · Grayson Highlands wild ponies. Mount Rogers 1746m — highest point in Virginia. 175 miles.

itinerary.overnight: Pickle Branch Shelter / The Home Place area
itinerary.mapNote: Section 5/8 · Central VA · McAfee Knob — most photographed spot on AT. Tinker Cliffs. Dragon's Tooth. The notorious Virginia blues. 170 miles.

itinerary.route: Shenandoah NP — Waynesboro to Front Royal
itinerary.mapNote: Section 6/8 · Shenandoah NP · 105 miles of Skyline Drive ridgeline. Waysides every 20 miles. Deer everywhere. Hawksbill Mountain 1235m.

itinerary.route: Mid-Atlantic — Front Royal to Connecticut Border
itinerary.overnight: Ed Garvey Shelter / Delaware Water Gap / Graymoor
itinerary.mapNote: Section 7/8 · WV / MD / PA / NJ / NY · Harpers Ferry emotional halfway. Pennsylvania Rocksylvania boulder fields. Bear Mountain NY. 430 miles.

itinerary.overnight: Lake of the Clouds Hut / Katahdin Stream Campground
itinerary.mapNote: Section 8/8 · New England · Presidential Range — Mount Washington 1917m. NH Whites most brutal terrain. Maine 100-Mile Wilderness. Katahdin finish. 630 miles.

waypoints.name: Springer Mountain
waypoints.note: Southern terminus. The bronze plaque where every NOBO thru-hike begins.

waypoints.name: Blood Mountain
waypoints.note: Highest point in Georgia on the AT. Rocky exposed summit with stone shelter.

waypoints.name: Clingmans Dome
waypoints.note: Highest point on the entire Appalachian Trail. Concrete observation tower.

waypoints.name: Roan Mountain
waypoints.note: Vast grassy balds above treeline. Rhododendron tunnels in June. Roan High Knob shelter at 1915m.

waypoints.name: Grayson Highlands
waypoints.note: Wild ponies roam the highland plateau freely. One of the AT's most magical landscapes.

waypoints.name: McAfee Knob
waypoints.note: The most photographed location on the entire AT. Iconic overhanging rock ledge.

waypoints.name: Harpers Ferry
waypoints.note: ATC headquarters. Emotional psychological halfway point for NOBO hikers. Historic Civil War town.

waypoints.name: Mount Washington
waypoints.note: Home of the world's worst recorded weather. Lake of the Clouds AMC hut just below summit.

waypoints.name: Katahdin — Baxter Peak
waypoints.note: The northern terminus. The wooden sign. The end of 2,190 miles. Baxter State Park.

itinerary.route: Southern California Desert — Campo to Kennedy Meadows
itinerary.mapNote: Section 1/5 · SoCal Desert · Mexican border at Campo. Mojave heat, 20-mile water carries. San Jacinto 3302m. Vasquez Rocks. 702 miles.

itinerary.route: High Sierra — Kennedy Meadows to South Lake Tahoe
itinerary.overnight: Rae Lakes / Tyndall Creek / Muir Trail Ranch
itinerary.mapNote: Section 2/5 · High Sierra · Forester Pass 4009m — highest point on PCT. Full JMT overlap. Muir Hut. Bear canisters mandatory. The crown of the trail. 315 miles.

itinerary.route: Northern California — South Lake Tahoe to Oregon Border
itinerary.overnight: Drakesbad Guest Ranch / Burney Falls State Park
itinerary.mapNote: Section 3/5 · NorCal Cascades · Desolation Wilderness. Lassen Volcanic NP. Burney Falls. Long resupply gaps. Active fire risk. 675 miles.

itinerary.route: Oregon Cascade Crest — Oregon Border to Bridge of the Gods
itinerary.overnight: Timberline Lodge / Mazama Village / Cascade Locks
itinerary.mapNote: Section 4/5 · Oregon · Crater Lake rim walk. Three Sisters Wilderness. Mount Hood. Timberline Lodge. Oregon's fast, flat miles — the 'Oregon bubble'. 456 miles.

itinerary.route: Washington North Cascades — Bridge of the Gods to Manning Park
itinerary.overnight: Stehekin / Glacier Peak camps / Manning Park Lodge
itinerary.mapNote: Section 5/5 · Washington · Goat Rocks knife-edge. Glacier Peak Wilderness. North Cascades. Monument 78 at Canadian border. The hardest and most spectacular state. 506 miles.

waypoints.name: Campo Southern Terminus
waypoints.note: Monument 1 at the Mexican border wall. The NOBO start for most PCT hikers.

waypoints.name: San Jacinto Peak
waypoints.note: Optional aerial-tram-accessed peak above Palm Springs. One of California's finest viewpoints.

waypoints.name: Kennedy Meadows
waypoints.note: End of the desert, start of the Sierra. Bear canisters become mandatory. Resupply and rest.

waypoints.name: Forester Pass
waypoints.note: Highest point on the entire PCT. Steep snow chutes in early season. Notched into a granite wall.

waypoints.name: Muir Trail Ranch
waypoints.note: Remote High Sierra resupply. No road access — float buckets pre-sent by hikers.

waypoints.name: Crater Lake
waypoints.note: The deepest lake in North America. Impossibly blue. Rim walk at 2000m with full crater views.

waypoints.name: Timberline Lodge
waypoints.note: Historic Mount Hood lodge. Hot meals, beds, and the famous PCT hiker welcome since 1937.

waypoints.name: Goat Rocks Wilderness
waypoints.note: The knife-edge ridge above glaciers with Mount Rainier dominating. The finest day in Washington.

waypoints.name: Monument 78
waypoints.note: The northern terminus at the Canadian border. Manning Park 13 miles beyond on a dirt road.

itinerary.route: New Mexico — Crazy Cook Monument to Cumbres Pass
itinerary.overnight: Gila River Camps / Silver City / Ghost Ranch
itinerary.mapNote: Section 1/5 · New Mexico · Southern terminus Crazy Cook. Gila Cliff Dwellings hot springs. El Malpais lava fields. Most poorly-marked section. 820 miles.

itinerary.route: Colorado — Cumbres Pass to Wyoming Border
itinerary.mapNote: Section 2/5 · Colorado · The crown — Grays Peak 4352m highest point on CDT. 30+ passes above 3600m. San Juan & Weminuche Wilderness. Maximum lightning exposure. 740 miles.

itinerary.route: Wyoming — Wyoming Border to Yellowstone North
itinerary.overnight: Cirque of the Towers / Pinedale / Yellowstone camps
itinerary.mapNote: Section 3/5 · Wyoming · Wind River Range — most spectacular CDT section. Cirque of the Towers. Great Divide Basin waterless crossing. Yellowstone geysers. 500 miles.
Section 4/5 · Bitterroots & Centennials · Lemhi Pass — Lewis and Clark 1805. Centennial Mountains. Trail poorly defined. Remote and self-reliant section. 350 miles.

itinerary.route: Montana — Lima to Waterton Lake (Northern Terminus)
itinerary.mapNote: Section 5/5 · Montana · Bob Marshall Wilderness — largest roadless area lower 48. Glacier NP. Chief Mountain border crossing. Waterton Lake northern terminus. 690 miles.

waypoints.name: Crazy Cook Monument
waypoints.note: Southern terminus. A lonely monument in the New Mexico desert. The NOBO start of the CDT.

waypoints.name: Gila Cliff Dwellings
waypoints.note: 13th-century Mogollon cliff dwellings. The only wilderness hot springs on the entire CDT.

waypoints.name: Grays Peak
waypoints.note: Highest point on the CDT. A 14er. 360° view over the Colorado Rockies.

waypoints.name: Cirque of the Towers
waypoints.note: Wind River Range granite amphitheatre. One of the great wilderness campsites in North America.

waypoints.name: Great Divide Basin
waypoints.note: Hydrological anomaly — water neither flows to Atlantic nor Pacific. 100-mile waterless alternate.

waypoints.name: Yellowstone Geysers
waypoints.note: Old Faithful and the thermal fields. The CDT passes through the world's largest supervolcano caldera.

waypoints.name: Lemhi Pass
waypoints.note: Lewis and Clark's Continental Divide crossing on 12 August 1805. The CDT follows their exact route.

waypoints.name: Bob Marshall Wilderness
waypoints.note: The largest roadless area in the contiguous US. Grizzly bears, wolves, no roads for 100+ miles.

waypoints.name: Waterton Lake
waypoints.note: Northern terminus at the US-Canada border. Chief Mountain Customs. Waterton Townsite beyond.

itinerary.route: Northland — Cape Reinga to Mangawhai Heads
itinerary.overnight: Ninety Mile Beach camps / Mangawhai DOC campsite
itinerary.mapNote: Section 1/10 · Northland · Cape Reinga lighthouse — tip of Aotearoa. Te Paki sand dunes. 90-mile beach road walk. Waipoua Kauri forest.

itinerary.route: Auckland Region — Mangawhai to Mercer via Auckland
itinerary.mapNote: Section 2/10 · Auckland · Urban traverse of NZ's largest city. Volcanic cones — One Tree Hill, Mount Eden. The TA passes through city streets.

itinerary.route: Waikato & King Country — Mercer to Taumarunui
itinerary.mapNote: Section 3/10 · Waikato · Hauraki Rail Trail. Timber Trail on converted railway. Hauhungaroa Range dense bush. Long section through farmland and forest.

itinerary.route: Whanganui River Journey — Taumarunui to Whanganui city
itinerary.overnight: River camps / John Coull Hut / Pipiriki Lodge
itinerary.mapNote: Section 4/10 · Whanganui River · 5 days by canoe or kayak down the sacred Whanganui. One of NZ's Great Journeys. Bridge to Nowhere side trip.

itinerary.route: Central Plateau — Whanganui to Tongariro Crossing
itinerary.mapNote: Section 5/10 · Tongariro · The Alpine Crossing — most-walked day hike in NZ. Mount Ngauruhoe (Mount Doom). Red Crater 1967m. Emerald Lakes.

itinerary.route: Tararua Ranges — Palmerston North to Wellington
itinerary.overnight: Tararua huts / Wellington city / Interislander ferry
itinerary.mapNote: Section 6/10 · Tararua Ranges · Hardest North Island section. Extreme weather. Mitre 1571m. Wellington ferry crossing to South Island.

itinerary.route: Marlborough & Nelson — Picton to St Arnaud
itinerary.overnight: Queen Charlotte huts / Richmond Range huts
itinerary.mapNote: Section 7/10 · Marlborough · Queen Charlotte Track along the Sounds. Richmond Ranges exposed ridge walking. Mount Richmond 1760m.

itinerary.route: Nelson Lakes & St James — St Arnaud to Boyle Village
itinerary.overnight: St James Walkway huts / Boyle Lodge
itinerary.mapNote: Section 8/10 · Nelson Lakes · Alpine St James high country. Tussock passes. Merino sheep stations. The Canterbury high country character changes the trail.

itinerary.route: Southern Alps — Arthur's Pass to Queenstown
itinerary.mapNote: Section 9/10 · Southern Alps · Avalanche Peak 1833m. Brewster Glacier. Haast Pass. Mount Aspiring NP. Most technical section — river crossings, route-finding essential.
Section 10/10 · Southland · Mavora Lakes tussock. The Southland flats. Bluff — the southern terminus. The famous directional signpost. End of 3000km.

waypoints.name: Cape Reinga
waypoints.note: The northern terminus — the very tip of New Zealand. Where the Tasman Sea and Pacific Ocean meet.

waypoints.name: Ninety Mile Beach
waypoints.note: 90 miles of hard-sand beach. Tide timing critical. The TA's most monotonous and iconic section.

waypoints.name: Mount Ngauruhoe
waypoints.note: The perfect volcanic cone. Mount Doom from Lord of the Rings. Side trip off the main TA route.

waypoints.name: Red Crater
waypoints.note: Highest point on the Tongariro Alpine Crossing. Vivid red-ochre volcanic landscape.

waypoints.name: Whanganui River
waypoints.note: Sacred Māori river — the first river in the world granted legal personhood. Canoe section of the TA.

waypoints.name: Brewster Glacier
waypoints.note: One of the most accessible Southern Alps glaciers. Views down the Haast River valley.

waypoints.name: Bluff
waypoints.note: The southern terminus. The famous signpost with distances to everywhere. 3000km from Cape Reinga.

itinerary.route: Kanchenjunga Section — Taplejung to Pangpema BC
itinerary.mapNote: Section 1/12 · Kanchenjunga · Eastern Nepal start. Terraced farmland to Tibetan-culture villages. Kanchenjunga North BC 5140m — world's 3rd highest.

itinerary.route: Kanchenjunga South & Yalung — BC Traverse to Dobhan
itinerary.mapNote: Section 2/12 · Kanchenjunga South · Sele La Pass 4290m. Yalung Glacier. Kanchenjunga South BC. Dense rhododendron descent.

itinerary.route: Makalu Section — Num to Makalu Base Camp
itinerary.mapNote: Section 3/12 · Makalu · Barun Valley — Nepal's most pristine wilderness corridor. Makalu 8463m overhead at BC.

itinerary.route: Khumbu Approach — Salleri to Kongma La
itinerary.mapNote: Section 4/12 · Khumbu · Kongma La 5535m — highest GHT high-route pass. Imja Glacier. Ama Dablam dominates skyline.

itinerary.route: Everest Section — Khumbu to Renjo La & Gokyo
itinerary.mapNote: Section 5/12 · Everest · Renjo La 5360m — finest viewpoint in the Himalaya: Everest, Lhotse, Makalu, Cho Oyu simultaneously. Gokyo sacred lakes.

itinerary.route: Rolwaling — Namche to Tesi Lapcha Pass & Simigaon
itinerary.mapNote: Section 6/12 · Rolwaling · Tesi Lapcha 5755m — hardest technical pass on GHT. Glacier travel, fixed ropes. Pristine rarely-visited Rolwaling Valley.

itinerary.route: Manaslu Circuit — Arughat to Larkya La
itinerary.mapNote: Section 7/12 · Manaslu · Larkya La 5213m. Manaslu 8163m directly overhead. Restricted area permit required. Classic circuit in superb condition.

itinerary.route: Annapurna Circuit — Dharapani to Thorong La
itinerary.mapNote: Section 8/12 · Annapurna · Thorong La 5416m — world's most-crossed high pass. Annapurna massif. Tilicho Lake 4919m optional detour.

itinerary.route: Upper Mustang — Muktinath to Lo Manthang
itinerary.mapNote: Section 9/12 · Mustang · The last forbidden kingdom. Tibet-like plateau landscape. Lo Manthang walled city. Sky caves carved into cliffs. Restricted area permit.

itinerary.route: Dolpo Section — Jomsom to Shey Gompa & Phoksundo Lake
itinerary.mapNote: Section 10/12 · Dolpo · The Snow Leopard country (Peter Matthiessen). Phoksundo Lake 3611m — Nepal's deepest. Highly restricted. One of the GHT's most profound sections.
Section 11/12 · Humla · Api Himal 7132m. The most remote section of the GHT. Almost no infrastructure. Tibetan border culture. Full self-sufficiency required.

itinerary.route: Western Nepal — Simikot to Hilsa (Tibet Border)
itinerary.mapNote: Section 12/12 · Western terminus · Hilsa at the Tibet border crossing. Karnali River. The end of the GHT high route — one of the world's most remote trail finishes.

waypoints.name: Taplejung
waypoints.note: Eastern terminus of the GHT high route. Gateway to Kanchenjunga.

waypoints.name: Kanchenjunga North BC
waypoints.note: Base camp of the world's third highest mountain. Among the most remote spots reachable on foot.

waypoints.name: Kongma La
waypoints.note: Highest pass on the GHT Khumbu section. Views encompass Makalu, Lhotse, Ama Dablam.

waypoints.name: Renjo La
waypoints.note: The finest viewpoint in the Himalaya — Everest, Lhotse, Makalu and Cho Oyu simultaneously visible.

waypoints.name: Tesi Lapcha Pass
waypoints.note: Hardest technical pass on the GHT. Glacier travel, fixed ropes, ice axe required.

waypoints.name: Larkya La
waypoints.note: Manaslu Circuit's famous high pass with Manaslu 8163m towering directly overhead.

waypoints.name: Thorong La
waypoints.note: World's most-trekked high-altitude pass. Annapurna Circuit centrepiece.

waypoints.name: Lo Manthang
waypoints.note: Walled capital of the former Kingdom of Mustang. Tibetan culture frozen in time.

waypoints.name: Phoksundo Lake
waypoints.note: Nepal's deepest lake. Otherworldly turquoise blue. The heart of Shey Phoksundo NP.

waypoints.name: Hilsa
waypoints.note: Western terminus of the GHT high route at the Tibetan border. End of one of the world's greatest treks.

itinerary.mapNote: Red Sea and Eilat Mountains. Desert from the first step. 300m.
Ramon Crater approaches. Negev desert. Ancient copper mines. 580m.
The Grand Canyon of Israel. Volcanic rocks and fossils. 1035m.
Ben Gurion's desert kibbutz. His grave and legacy. 450m.
Ancient biblical city. Abraham's wells. Transition to lowlands. 300m.
Footsteps of Samson and Delilah country. Rolling agricultural land. 200m.
Approach to Jerusalem through biblical landscape. 800m.
Armageddon plain. Carmel ridge ahead. 100m.
Carmel coast. Baha'i Gardens. Western Galilee coastal plain. 547m.
Mount Hermon views. Tel Dan archaeological site. Trail complete. 700m.

waypoints.name: Makhtesh Ramon
waypoints.note: Largest erosion crater in the world. 40km long, 400m deep.

waypoints.name: Jerusalem
waypoints.note: The trail passes through or near Jerusalem's ancient walls.

waypoints.name: Sea of Galilee
waypoints.note: Lowest freshwater lake on earth. Jesus walked these shores.

waypoints.name: Mount Hermon
waypoints.note: Highest point in Israel. Snow-capped most of the year.

itinerary.mapNote: Leave Oviedo cathedral. The Original Way begins in the ancient Asturian capital. 300m.
Rolling Asturian countryside. Green hills and village churches. 400m.
Altitude increases. Atlantic Spain shifting toward mountain Spain. 600m.
Deep Asturian valleys. Ancient Roman road sections. 700m.

itinerary.route: Pola de Allande to La Mesa
itinerary.mapNote: Into the high Cantabrian range. The toughest section begins. 1000m.
Cross into Galicia. Language changes, cuisine changes. 900m.
Descent to Roman Lugo — complete Roman walls intact. 600m.
Junction with the Camino Francés. Pulpo a la gallega capital. 450m.
Final hill before Santiago. First cathedral glimpse. 350m.

itinerary.route: Monte do Gozo to Santiago de Compostela
itinerary.mapNote: Arrive at the Cathedral. Pilgrim Mass. Abrazo of Saint James. 260m.

waypoints.name: Oviedo Cathedral
waypoints.note: The original starting point. King Alfonso II first walked this in 830 AD.

waypoints.name: Cruz de los Caminos
waypoints.note: Highest point on the Primitivo. Ancient Celtic cross at the summit.

waypoints.name: Lugo Roman Walls
waypoints.note: UNESCO World Heritage Roman walls. Only complete Roman circuit in the world.

waypoints.name: Santiago Cathedral
waypoints.note: The final destination. Gold baroque facade. Botafumeiro incense ceremony.

itinerary.route: Dempster Highway TH to Grizzly Lake
itinerary.mapNote: First tundra views open up immediately. Grizzly Lake sits below the Ogilvie ridgeline. 1420m.

itinerary.route: Grizzly Lake to Divide Lake via Grizzly Col
itinerary.mapNote: Grizzly Col at 1650m — the route's high point. Divide Lake below is the classic Tombstone image. 1650m.
Off-trail descent to Talus Lake beneath Tombstone Mountain's jagged face. 1540m.

itinerary.route: Talus Lake to Goldensides Loop and return to TH
itinerary.overnight: Return to Dawson City or Whitehorse
itinerary.mapNote: Goldensides viewpoint — full Tombstone panorama. Autumn tundra colour at its peak in late August. 1580m.

waypoints.name: Tombstone Mountain
waypoints.note: The jagged summit that defines the park — not a trekking summit but always visible.

waypoints.name: Grizzly Col
waypoints.note: High point of the backpacking circuit. Sweeping tundra panoramas in all directions.

waypoints.name: Divide Lake
waypoints.note: The most photographed spot in Tombstone — turquoise lake below jagged peaks.

waypoints.name: Talus Lake
waypoints.note: Remote high lake beneath boulder fields and the north face of the Ogilvie Mountains.

waypoints.name: Goldensides
waypoints.note: Named for autumn tundra colour — a viewpoint above the Dempster Highway with full park panorama.

itinerary.route: Berg Lake TH to Kinney Lake
itinerary.mapNote: Flat valley walk beside the Robson River. Kinney Lake turquoise water backed by Mount Robson's lower walls. 1050m.

itinerary.route: Kinney Lake to Berg Lake via Valley of a Thousand Falls
itinerary.mapNote: Emperor Falls at 67m, Robson Falls, and Whitehorn Falls — three major cascades in quick succession. Berg Lake arrival. 1646m.

itinerary.route: Berg Lake day exploration — Hargreaves Glacier Meadows
itinerary.mapNote: Hargreaves Meadows above the lake — full view of Mount Robson and the Berg Glacier calving into the lake. 2100m.

itinerary.route: Berg Lake to Trailhead — full return
itinerary.mapNote: Descent past all falls again in reverse. Mount Robson's summit often revealed on morning return. 856m TH.

waypoints.name: Mount Robson Summit
waypoints.note: Highest peak in the Canadian Rockies — often cloud-capped. A clear sighting is rare and celebrated.

waypoints.name: Emperor Falls
waypoints.note: 67-metre waterfall — the trail passes directly beside the cascade on a narrow ledge trail.

waypoints.name: Berg Lake
waypoints.note: Turquoise glacial lake with icebergs calved from the Berg Glacier. Mount Robson rises directly above.

waypoints.name: Berg Glacier
waypoints.note: Active glacier calving directly into Berg Lake — watch for iceberg falls from the camp or meadows.

waypoints.name: Hargreaves Glacier Meadows
waypoints.note: The finest viewpoint on the trail — Mount Robson, Berg Glacier, and the lake all visible simultaneously.

waypoints.name: Kinney Lake
waypoints.note: Turquoise lake at the foot of Mount Robson's approach walls — striking on arrival and departure.

itinerary.route: Paraitepui village to Rio Kukenan camp
itinerary.mapNote: Flat savanna approach across the Gran Sabana. Kukenan tepui visible ahead. 1300m.

itinerary.route: Rio Kukenan to base of Roraima — La Rampa foot
itinerary.mapNote: Roraima's walls grow from horizon to sky. Camp at the foot of the only natural ascent route. 1600m.

itinerary.route: La Rampa ascent to summit plateau
itinerary.mapNote: La Rampa — the 45-degree forested ramp, the only non-technical route to the top. Summit plateau at 2810m.

itinerary.route: Summit plateau exploration — El Foso, Triple Point, Crystal Valley
itinerary.mapNote: El Foso circular canyon, Triple Point border marker, black rock labyrinths and quartzite fields. 2810m.

itinerary.route: Descent La Rampa to savanna camp
itinerary.mapNote: Full descent of La Rampa. Views back to the tepui walls from the savanna floor. 1400m.

itinerary.overnight: Return to Santa Elena de Uairén
itinerary.mapNote: Return across the Gran Sabana. Final views of Roraima and Kukenan from the trail. 1050m.

waypoints.name: La Rampa
waypoints.note: The only natural non-technical ascent — a forested ramp on the tepui's southwest face.

waypoints.name: El Foso
waypoints.note: A deep circular canyon cut into the summit plateau — one of Roraima's most dramatic geological features.

waypoints.name: Triple Point
waypoints.note: The convergence point of Venezuela, Brazil, and Guyana — stand in three countries simultaneously.

waypoints.name: Crystal Valley
waypoints.note: Quartz crystal fields on the summit plateau — the source of the Lost World mythology.

waypoints.name: Kukenan Tepui
waypoints.note: Roraima's near-twin tepui, visible throughout the approach across the Gran Sabana.

waypoints.name: Roraima Summit Plateau
waypoints.note: An alien world of black rock formations, carnivorous plants, and endemic species found nowhere else on Earth.
